target.name: Ulysses Robotics
target.founder: Akhil Voorakkara, Jamie Wedderburn, Colm O'Brien, Will O'Brien

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $38M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Ulysses Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $38M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private robotics firms and 1 public peer, spanning similar and adjacent funding stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $38M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implying a $76M valuation. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: 1X Technologies ($10.0B Series B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M Series B), and Fairmat ($350M Series B). This cohort yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range of $300M to $10.0B.

The two methods produce a triangulated range of $65M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $576M. This wide spread reflects material stage and outcome heterogeneity within the robotics peer set: the stage-progression method anchors conservatively to current capital raised, while peer-median pulls substantially higher given the valuations commanded by Series B robotics firms. The key uncertainty driving range width is whether Ulysses Robotics' trajectory and market position will approximate lower-quartile peers like Fairmat or command multiples closer to sector leaders. Earlier-stage positioning and lack of public comparables further limit precision.
